titleStmt.title: Letter from William Edward Thrift to Sir Matthew Nathan, 29 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Edward Thrift

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Fellow of Trinity College Dublin, Professor William Edward Thrift (1870-1947), to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), the under-secretary for Ireland. Nathan was under-secretary until the 1916 Rising after which he offered his resignation. In this letter Thrift is bringing to Nathan's attention a Dublin newspaper named 'Eye-Opener' and claims that it is making terrible insinuations about some of the members of his Rathmines Parish Church. Thrift claims that the people being targeted are good people and that the paper is causing serious harm. He is calling for Nathan to act against the paper using the Defence of the Realm Act.Mathew Nathan had become under-secretary in 1914 under Augustine Birrell (1850-1933). After the Easter Rising 1916, Nathan resigned as under-secretary due to his misjudgement and failure to stop the Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5561/3

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
80, Grosvenor Square Rathmines Dublin 29th March 1916 The Rt. Hon. Sir Mathew Nathan G.C.M.G. The Under Secretary Dear Sir The serious and urgent
nature of the matter
about which I write is my apology
for troubling you with this
letter. A paper called the
'Eye -opener', published in Dublin
and publicly sold has been
brought under my notice, owing
its scandalous and
malicious insinuations, against
some of the members of the
congragation of Rathmines Parish
Church, and even our Rector.
The attacks are so thinly
veiled that no one in the
district is in doubt as to
the identity of those to whom
reference is made, and I
may say that they are old
and respected members of
the congregation. One of them
has been Hon. Secretary of the
select Vestry of the Parish for
years. Needless to say, the charges
are absolutely without foundation,
but great pain is being caused
to worthy people, both ladies and gentlemen, and much have may
be, and i fear is being, done
by the circulation of such
statements and the gossip which
they incite Civil proceeding are
useless to you to take steps to
suppress the paper, if this be
possible you will be confessing
a boon on the communities of
Rathmines and Dublin by stopping
a grave scandal. I have been a member
of the congregation for some
30 years and of its select Vestry
some 20 years and know well those
whose characters are being defamed. I regard
the matter as really anxious and feel the
deepest indignation at it. I am sure you
will, under the circumstances, pardon me for
thus bringing it under your notice. Yours faithfully, W mL Thrift
P.T.C.D.
